arts.sum: Crystal Palace host Brighton in the Premier League on Saturday. Preview includes team news, predictions and how to watch on TV.

arts.ttl: Report: Sensational stoppage time strikes see Palace topple West Ham
arts.sum: Crystal Palace Under-21s came back from being a goal down at half-time to win 2-5 away at West Ham United on a warm April evening at Rush Green. Three late goals in stoppage time, following two earlier equalisers in the second-half saw Palace leave East London with all three points in the bag.

arts.ttl: Crystal Palace boss confirms youngster has penned fresh terms and makes Daniel Munoz future admission
arts.sum: “He isn’t in the squad tomorrow, he’s with the U21s, because he needs minutes to play for his personal development. “He did,” said Glasner when asked if Kporha had signed a new deal at Selhurst Park.

arts.ttl: Glasner reveals pre-Brighton message to his players
arts.sum: Oliver Glasner says the allure of possibly writing history will add even further to his Crystal Palace players’ determination to win against Brighton & Hove Albion on Saturday afternoon.

arts.ttl: Matt O'Riley mentor senses Celtic return is ON if mooted mega money exit gets the green light
arts.sum: O'Riley exited Glasgow last summer in a deal which could rise to a Scottish record £30m but he's toiled at Brighton

arts.ttl: Glasner and Hürzeler set friendship aside as Palace ready for Brighton test
arts.sum: Crystal Palace out to earn first double over rivals since 1933 in ‘A23 derby’ that has import beyond bragging rights When Fabian Hürzeler called Oliver Glasner a few days after his appointment by Brighton last June, he found the Crystal Palace manager was only too happy to offer a few pointers about life in the Premier League. “He is very helpful and open-minded,” Hürzeler later said. “He is a role model, I think, in how he acts and how humble and grounded he is.” Although Glasner and Hürzeler were managers in Germany at the same time at Eintracht Frankfurt and St Pauli respectively, they worked in different divisions and had not come face to face until Ismaïla Sarr scored twice in a 3-1 Palace victory at the Amex in December. The Austrian had warned his Brighton counterpart in one of their frequent chats that their friendship would be put aside for the afternoon as arch-rival clubs met in the fixture some outsiders have taken to referring to as the “A23 derby”. Continue reading...

arts.ttl: “I had a great time” – Player loved Chelsea and Crystal Palace spells, can’t wait to take on Tottenham
arts.sum: Michy Batshuayi has insisted that he loved his time in London with Chelsea and Crystal Palace and is now looking forward to a return to take on Tottenham. The striker has been speaking to Eintracht Frankfurt’s official website as the gear up to take on Tottenham in the Europa League quarterfinals. Eintracht travel to Tottenham The post “I had a great time” – Player loved Chelsea and Crystal Palace spells, can’t wait to take on Tottenham appeared first on Sport Witness.
